_meta.title: NOVIQ Clinician Query Templates
_meta.authority: ACS 0010 — Coding from Incomplete or Unclear Documentation

pdx_triggers.description: Only symptom documented, no confirmed dx

pdx_query_diagnosis.title: Principal Diagnosis Confirmation Required
pdx_query_diagnosis.body:
The discharge documentation uses the term '{uncertain_term}' before the diagnosis of '{diagnosis}'. Under ACS 0001, the Principal Diagnosis must be the condition established AFTER study to be chiefly responsible for the episode of care.

Could you please clarify: Was '{diagnosis}' confirmed as the principal diagnosis at the conclusion of this episode? If so, please document as a confirmed diagnosis in the Discharge Summary.

pdx_probable.title: Diagnosis Certainty — Probable vs Confirmed
pdx_probable.body:
The documentation describes '{diagnosis}' as '{uncertain_term}' (e.g., probable/likely/possible). Under ICD-10-AM guidelines, probable/likely/possible diagnoses in the inpatient setting MAY be coded as confirmed.

Could you please confirm: At discharge, was '{diagnosis}' your working confirmed diagnosis, or does significant uncertainty remain?
physician_options: Remains probable only — code as stated
pdx_probable.coding_impact: Affects PDX selection and DRG grouping

pdx_rule_out.title: Rule Out Diagnosis — Clarification Required
pdx_rule_out.body:
The documentation states 'rule out {diagnosis}'. Under ICD-10-AM, a 'rule out' diagnosis that was NOT confirmed should be coded as the presenting symptom or sign.

Could you please confirm: Was '{diagnosis}' ruled in or ruled out during this episode?
physician_options: Ruled OUT — code presenting symptom instead
pdx_rule_out.coding_impact: Critical — determines if condition is coded or symptom used instead

pdx_competing.title: Competing Diagnoses — Principal Diagnosis Selection
pdx_competing.body:
The documentation presents two competing diagnoses: '{diagnosis_a}' vs '{diagnosis_b}'.

Under ACS 0001, when two or more conditions equally meet the criteria for PDX, the condition that consumed the most resources should be selected.

Could you please indicate which condition was the PRIMARY reason for this admission and consumed the most resources?
physician_options: Both equally — coder to select per ACS 0001
pdx_competing.coding_impact: Determines MDC, AR-DRG, and DRG weight

pdx_symptom_only.trigger: Only symptom documented, no confirmed diagnosis
pdx_symptom_only.title: Underlying Diagnosis — Symptom Documentation
pdx_symptom_only.body:
The documentation records '{symptom}' as the principal condition. Under ACS 0011, if an underlying condition is identified during the episode, it should be coded as PDX rather than the symptom.

Was an underlying diagnosis identified to explain '{symptom}'? If so, please document in the Discharge Summary.
physician_options: No — symptom remains the principal condition
pdx_symptom_only.coding_impact: Symptom as PDX may result in lower DRG weight

adx_history_of.title: Active vs Historical Condition
adx_history_of.body:
The documentation mentions 'history of {condition}'. Under ACS 0002, a condition should only be coded as an Additional Diagnosis if it affected patient care during this episode.

Did '{condition}' impact this admission through: (1) altered therapeutic treatment, (2) diagnostic investigation, or (3) increased clinical monitoring?
physician_options: Yes — actively managed during this admission
No — background history only, did not impact care
adx_history_of.coding_impact: ACS 0002 scoring — affects ADX inclusion and DRG complexity

adx_known_condition.title: Known Condition — Impact on This Admission
adx_known_condition.body:
The documentation notes '{condition}' as a known comorbidity. To meet ACS 0002 criteria for coding, this condition must have impacted care.

During this admission, was '{condition}' managed differently from the patient's baseline (e.g., medication adjusted, additional monitoring, or investigation performed)?
physician_options: No — stable, no change from baseline
adx_known_condition.coding_impact: Determines whether condition is coded as ADX

adx_elevated_value.trigger: elevated lab value without named diagnosis
adx_elevated_value.title: Elevated {value_name} — Clinical Significance
adx_elevated_value.body:
The results show an elevated {value_name} ({value}) without a documented clinical diagnosis.

Could you please clarify: Does this finding represent a diagnosable condition (e.g., hyperkalaemia, hyperglycaemia, elevated creatinine = AKI)? If so, please document the clinical diagnosis.
physician_options: Being monitored — not yet a diagnosis
adx_elevated_value.coding_impact: Specific diagnosis codes carry higher ACS 0002 scores than abnormal finding codes

adx_borderline.title: Borderline Condition — Clinical Diagnosis
adx_borderline.body:
The documentation describes '{condition}' as 'borderline'. Under ICD-10-AM, borderline conditions are coded using the specific borderline code or as the stated condition if clinical judgement supports it.

Please confirm: Should '{condition}' be treated as a confirmed diagnosis or coded as 'borderline' using the specific ICD-10-AM code?
adx_borderline.coding_impact: Affects code selection and DRG complexity level

adx_incidental_finding.title: Incidental Finding — Clinical Relevance
adx_incidental_finding.body:
An incidental finding of '{finding}' was noted. Under ACS 0002, incidental findings should only be coded if they impacted patient care.

Was any action taken regarding '{finding}' during this admission (investigation, treatment, referral, or increased monitoring)?
physician_options: Referral made for outpatient follow-up only
adx_incidental_finding.coding_impact: If no action taken — do not code per ACS 0002

proc_attempted.title: Attempted Procedure — ACS 0019 Clarification
proc_attempted.body:
The Operation Notes describe an '{procedure}' that was attempted but not completed. Under ACS 0019, an abandoned or incomplete procedure requires specific coding.

Could you please confirm: (1) At what point was the procedure abandoned? (2) Was anaesthesia administered? (3) Was an alternative procedure performed instead?
physician_options: Abandoned before anaesthesia — do not code procedure
Abandoned after anaesthesia but before incision
Abandoned during procedure — code with modifier
proc_attempted.coding_impact: ACS 0019 — abandoned procedure coding rules apply

proc_conversion.title: Procedure Conversion — Approach Clarification
proc_conversion.body:
The Operation Notes indicate conversion from laparoscopic to open '{procedure}'. Both approaches require separate ACHI codes.

Could you confirm: (1) Was the laparoscopic attempt coded separately? (2) What was the reason for conversion (adhesions, bleeding, technical difficulty)?
proc_conversion.coding_impact: Conversion = two ACHI codes + higher complexity ECCS

proc_laterality.title: Procedure Laterality — Left vs Right
proc_laterality.body:
The ACHI code for '{procedure}' requires specification of laterality (left/right/bilateral). The Operation Notes mention '{side}' but the ACHI modifier needs confirmation.

Please confirm the exact laterality performed.
proc_laterality.coding_impact: Bilateral vs unilateral = different ACHI modifier = different ECCS weight

proc_bilateral.title: Bilateral Procedure Confirmation
proc_bilateral.body:
The ACHI code for '{procedure}' may apply to one or both sides. The documentation does not explicitly confirm bilateral performance.

Was '{procedure}' performed bilaterally in this episode?
physician_options: Staged — first side only this admission

compl_postop_fever.title: Post-operative Fever — Complication vs Expected
compl_postop_fever.body:
Post-operative fever (T {temperature}°C) on Day {day} is documented. Under ACS 0017, post-procedural complications must be distinguished from expected post-operative findings.

Does this fever represent: (1) an expected post-operative response, or (2) a clinical complication requiring treatment (e.g., wound infection, pneumonia, anastomotic leak)?
compl_postop_fever.coding_impact: Confirmed complication = COF 1 + ACS 0017 + higher DRG complexity

compl_wound.title: Wound Issue — Complication Classification
compl_wound.body:
The nursing/progress notes document a wound-related finding. Under ACS 0017, wound complications are coded only when they represent a true complication requiring additional management.

How would you classify this wound finding?
compl_wound.coding_impact: True complication = COF 1 + significant DRG weight increase

compl_ileus.title: Post-operative Ileus — Paralytic vs Mechanical
compl_ileus.body:
Post-operative ileus is documented. Under ICD-10-AM, paralytic ileus (K56.0) and mechanical obstruction require different coding, and post-op ileus beyond expected recovery may represent a complication.

How would you classify this ileus?
physician_options: Expected post-op ileus — resolved within 48h, no complication
Prolonged paralytic ileus — required nasogastric tube/extended management
Ileus due to electrolyte imbalance — treat underlying cause
compl_ileus.coding_impact: Prolonged ileus = ACS 0017 complication code + COF 1

cof_hospital_acquired.trigger: condition noted only in progress notes, not admission report
cof_hospital_acquired.title: Condition Onset Flag — Hospital Acquired vs POA
cof_hospital_acquired.body:
The condition '{condition}' appears in the Progress Notes but was NOT documented at the time of admission. Under ACS 0048, we must determine if this condition was present on admission (COF 2) or arose during the admission (COF 1 — hospital acquired).

Was '{condition}' present before admission or did it develop during this episode?
cof_hospital_acquired.coding_impact: Hospital-acquired conditions trigger quality indicators and affect case complexity

specificity_diabetes.title: Diabetes — Type Specification Required
specificity_diabetes.body:
The documentation refers to 'diabetes' or 'DM' without specifying the type. ICD-10-AM has specific codes for Type 1 (E10) and Type 2 (E11) diabetes.

Could you please confirm the type of diabetes for accurate coding?
specificity_diabetes.coding_impact: Type 1 vs Type 2 = different ICD codes, different DRG complexity

specificity_sepsis.title: Sepsis — Causative Organism and Source
specificity_sepsis.body:
Sepsis is documented. Under ICD-10-AM, accurate coding requires: (1) the specific sepsis code, and (2) the underlying source/focus.

Could you please document: (1) the causative organism if identified, and (2) the primary source of infection (e.g., UTI, pneumonia, wound, biliary)?
specificity_sepsis.coding_impact: Specific sepsis + source = significantly higher DRG weight vs unspecified

priority_order: HIGH: Post-op complication vs observation → fire if fever/wound in progress notes
HIGH: Procedure abandoned or converted → fire if OT notes indicate
MEDIUM: ADX history-of or known → fire if no ACS 0002 evidence
MEDIUM: COF assignment unclear → fire if condition not in admission report
LOW: Specificity (diabetes type, sepsis source) → fire if unspecified
auto_suppress: Do not generate query if physician has already documented clear answer
Do not generate duplicate queries for same condition
Suppress LOW severity queries if episode has 3+ higher priority queries
